title: papaver rhoeas f. pinnatifidum
intro: Papaver rhoeas f. pinnatifidum, also known as the pink poppy or Shirley poppy, is a variety of the common corn poppy (Papaver rhoeas) that has been cultivated for its showy, pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as papaverine and thebaine. The medicinal properties of Papaver rhoeas f. pinnatifidum have been found to provide health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antitumor activities. Papaver rhoeas f. pinnatifidum is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin conditions.
